_comment: Example Claude Code MCP server configuration. This file shows how to configure the Apicurio Registry MCP server in your Claude Code settings (typically at ~/.claude/settings.json or in your project's .claude/settings.json).

apicurio-registry._comment: Primary Apicurio Registry MCP server — connects to a local development instance.

apicurio-registry-staging._comment: Staging registry — uses token-based authentication. The REGISTRY_TOKEN environment variable must be set in your shell before launching Claude Code.

apicurio-registry-production._comment: Production registry — read-only access. Even though the underlying credentials may have write permissions, the MCP server is configured to reject write operations at the server level.

_usage_notes.multiple_servers: You can configure multiple MCP servers pointing to different registry instances. Claude Code will prefix tool names with the server name when there are conflicts, e.g., 'apicurio-registry-staging__search_artifacts'.
_usage_notes.environment_variables: Tokens use ${VAR_NAME} syntax to reference environment variables from your shell. Never hardcode secrets in this file.
_usage_notes.read_only_mode: For production registries, always use --read-only mode. This prevents the AI agent from creating, updating, or deleting artifacts regardless of the underlying credentials.
_usage_notes.file_location: Place this configuration in ~/.claude/settings.json for global settings, or in your project's .claude/settings.json for project-specific settings.
